niche: Claude Fable 5 / AI coding tools & agentic development
note: Follow-up research after publishing claude-fable-5-developer-review-migration-guide. Topics avoid overlap with existing 110 posts.

topics.title: Fable 5 as Orchestrator: The Model-Routing Playbook for Claude Code
topics.whyTrending: Community-converged architecture post-relaunch: Fable 5 plans/reviews, Sonnet 5 builds, Haiku 4.5 does chores. Nobody shows a complete working setup with subagent configs and real cost numbers.

topics.title: The July 7 Cliff: Fable 5 Usage-Credit Economics for Heavy Users
topics.whyTrending: Today the 50% weekly-usage inclusion window ends; Fable 5 moves to usage credits. HN estimates put sustained heavy usage at 10-20x a Max subscription. Furious Reddit threads = huge search intent, zero calm math-driven coverage.

topics.title: Multi-Day Autonomous Agents with Fable 5 and Managed Agents (Outcomes + Rubrics)
topics.whyTrending: Fable 5's headline capability is days-long autonomous work; Managed Agents' define_outcome + rubric grading is the intended harness. Almost no hands-on content pairing the two.

topics.title: When Fable 5 Refuses Your Legit Code: Auditing Security-Adjacent Pipelines
topics.whyTrending: Post-redeploy classifiers false-positive on C/C++/Rust, Win32, 'unsafe', 'hook'. Developers report entire workloads silently downgraded to Opus 4.8. A practical audit + monitoring guide would own this pain point.

topics.title: Claude 5 Family Decision Framework: Fable 5 vs Opus 4.8 vs Sonnet 5 vs Haiku 4.5
topics.whyTrending: Four active tiers with overlapping capabilities and 10x price spread. 'Which Claude model should I use' is an evergreen high-volume query that spiked after Fable 5 GA.

topics.title: Refusal-Resilient LLM Pipelines: Server-Side Fallbacks, Fallback Credits, Sticky Routing
topics.whyTrending: The fallbacks API surface (server-side-fallback beta, fallback credit tokens, sticky routing) is brand new and undocumented outside Anthropic's reference. Deep-dive engineering content gap.

topics.title: Your Opus Prompts Are Hurting Fable 5: The De-Prescribing Guide
topics.whyTrending: Anthropic's own migration guide says over-prescriptive prior-model prompts reduce Fable 5 output quality. Counter-intuitive, shareable, and nobody has written the practical before/after version.

topics.title: Effort Parameter Sweep: Benchmarking Fable 5 at low/medium/high/xhigh/max on Real Tasks
topics.whyTrending: Effort is the only depth control on Fable 5 (thinking is always on). Anthropic claims low effort beats prior models' max. Original data content = strong differentiation and backlinks.

topics.title: Export Controls Hit an AI Model: Vendor-Risk Architecture After the Fable 5 Suspension
topics.whyTrending: First-ever export-control suspension of a commercial model (June 12 - July 1). Architecture lessons (model pinning, fallback config, multi-provider) for anyone building on frontier APIs.

topics.title: Fable 5 Vision Pipelines: Dense Documents, Degraded Screenshots, Real OCR Workloads
topics.whyTrending: State-of-the-art vision (beat Pokemon FireRed vision-only; trained to crop/tool-use on blurry inputs). Vision-pipeline content for devs is scarce vs coding content.
